result:
The kit is built. Here's what teenage.engineering is actually doing — and it's stranger and better than the usual answer.

## It isn't a responsive website. It's a poster that zooms.

Every dimension on that site — type size, padding, header height, button height — is written as a fraction of the viewport width, and every one of those fractions is *(some pixel value) ÷ 980*. There's a real 980px Swiss print layout underneath it: 12 columns of 65px, 10px gutters, 45px margins. That adds up to exactly 980. The browser just scales the whole thing like a vector. Nothing reflows on desktop; the page grows and shrinks as one object. It feels *drawn* rather than *assembled* because it was drawn once, at one size.

**The type is measured in baselines, not points.** The core text rule computes its own font size from the grid: font-size is `2 baselines − 2px`, line-height is *exactly* 2 baselines. The 10px baseline is the atom, and type gets snapped onto it instead of the layout being nudged to fit the type. Every line-height in the system (10, 15, 20, 30, 40) is a multiple of it.

**Nothing is heavy, round, or moving.** The interface face ships in two weights only — Thin 100 and Light 300 — and nothing is bolder than Light. Body copy sits at 100. Where a modern site would use a variable font's `opsz` axis, they ship two separately drawn cuts (`te-20` for small text, `te-40` for large) and switch font files by size — hand-cut optical sizing. Corners are a hard `0`. Borders are 0.75px hairlines and there are zero drop shadows: surfaces separate with *rules*, like a technical drawing. The entire production CSS contains exactly **one** cubic-bezier — there's no easing system because there's essentially no motion. That restraint is what lets the single orange accent and the giant black headline hit as hard as they do.

One honest deviation: the skill's template assumes the source uses a variable font and tells you to hunt for an `opsz` axis, weight 450, and pixel line-heights. None of that is here. `process.md` step 5 says to drop those when the source isn't variable, so I did, and documented the three real decisions instead.

## The kit

In `opus-trials/teenage-engineering-kit/` (Desktop needed a permission grant, and this is where the level-7 build happens anyway):

- **`builder-brief.md`** — paste-ready for any builder. Tokens, the 7-section structure translated into TE's actual grammar (icon-led nav, poster hero, hairline-divided tiles, a spec table, one marquee), house rules, and acceptance criteria.
- **`tokens.json`** — 152 custom properties, categorized, each with both the verbatim `calc()` and its resolved pixel value at 980.
- **`scaffold.html`** — working HTML implementing every rule, with `{{slots}}` for your copy. Open it and resize the window; you'll watch the poster scale.
- **`preview.png`** — the source screenshot for A/B.

For the truck site, the single highest-leverage move is the `--px` generator: never write a raw pixel in a layout rule, only `calc(N * var(--px))`. That one habit reproduces most of the feel. Your pink becomes the ink — it replaces `#f05a24` and appears on about three elements per screen, no more.

I left `{{BRAND}}` and `{{ACCENT}}` unfilled rather than assuming. Want me to fill them with CYBR and your pink and generate the finished `pink-cybertruck-web-level-7.html`?

tool_input.content:
{
  "_meta": {
    "source": "https://teenage.engineering/",
    "extracted": "2026-07-12",
    "note": "Every size on this site is expressed as a fraction of viewport width, where the fraction is (px at a 980px design width) / 980. 1/980 = 0.0010204081632653062. The 'px_at_980' fields below are the resolved design-time pixel values; the 'value' fields are verbatim from the production CSS.",
    "base_design_width": 980,
    "client_width": "100vw"
  },

  "palette": {
    "--white": "#fff",
    "--te-white": "#f5f5f5",
    "--te-black": "#0f0e12",
    "--te-grey-100": "#e5e5e5",
    "--te-grey-200": "#ccc",
    "--te-grey-300": "#b2b2b2",
    "--te-grey-500": "#a1a7af",
    "--te-grey-600": "#767676",
    "--te-grey-700": "#7f7f7f",
    "--te-grey-900": "#4d4d4d",
    "--te-grey-1000": "#272727",
    "--te-blue": "#0071bb",
    "--te-green": "#006837",
    "--te-orange": "#f05a24",
    "--te-red": "#b81d13",
    "--te-yellow": "#fab413",
    "--success": "var(--te-green)",
    "--error": "#c0262c"
  },

  "type": {
    "_families": {
      "--te-20": "\"te-20\", \"Unicode\", sans-serif",
      "--te-40": "\"te-40\", \"Unicode\", sans-serif",
      "_note": "te-20 and te-40 are two SEPARATE cuts of the same Univers-derived typeface (files: TE20T/TE20L, TE40T/TE40L). te-20 is the small-text cut; te-40 is the large-text cut. This is hand-drawn optical sizing done with two font files instead of a variable `opsz` axis.",
      "_display": "franxurter (poster face, weight 300) and TechnoType (400/500/700) carry the giant headline; both are loaded with font-display:block so the poster never flashes a fallback."
    },
    "_weights": {
      "--fw-thin": "100",
      "--fw-light": "300",
      "_note": "These are the ONLY two weights in the interface type system. Nothing on the site is bolder than Light 300. Body copy defaults to 100."
    },
    "_font_sizes_desktop": {
      "--fs-10": { "value": "calc(.00918367 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 9 },
      "--fs-15": { "value": "calc(.0132653 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 13 },
      "--fs-20": { "value": "calc(.0183673 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 18 },
      "--fs-25": { "value": "calc(.0234694 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 23 },
      "--fs-30": { "value": "calc(.027551 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 27 },
      "--fs-40": { "value": "calc(.0367347 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 36 }
    },
    "_line_heights_desktop": {
      "--lh-10": { "value": "calc(.0102041 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 10 },
      "--lh-15": { "value": "calc(.0153061 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 15 },
      "--lh-20": { "value": "calc(.0204082 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 20 },
      "--lh-25": { "value": "calc(.0306122 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 30 },
      "--lh-30": { "value": "calc(.0306122 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 30 },
      "--lh-40": { "value": "calc(.0408163 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 40 },
      "_note": "Every line-height is a multiple of the 10px baseline. Type sits ON the grid; the grid is never adjusted to fit the type."
    },
    "_font_sizes_mobile": {
      "_note": "Mobile does not reflow — it re-scales. The same tokens are redefined with ~2x fractions so the 980px design reads at phone width.",
      "--fs-10": "calc(.0183673 * var(--client-width))",
      "--fs-15": "calc(.0265306 * var(--client-width))",
      "--fs-20": "calc(.0367347 * var(--client-width))",
      "--fs-25": "calc(.0469388 * var(--client-width))",
      "--fs-30": "calc(.055102 * var(--client-width))",
      "--fs-40": "calc(.0734694 * var(--client-width))"
    },
    "_txt_engine": {
      "--txt-px-unit": "calc(0.0010204081632653062 * var(--client-width))",
      "--txt-bh-unit": "calc(0.010204081632653062 * var(--client-width))",
      "--txt-letter-spacing": "calc(var(--txt-px-unit) * -.5 * var(--txt-scale))",
      "--txt-scale": "var(--txt-local-scale,1)",
      "--txt-word-break": "keep-all",
      "_the_rule": ".txt { font-size: calc((var(--txt-bh-unit) * 2 - var(--txt-px-unit) * 2) * var(--txt-scale)); line-height: calc(var(--txt-bh-unit) * 2 * var(--txt-scale)); font-weight: 100; letter-spacing: var(--txt-letter-spacing); }",
      "_translation": "font-size = (2 baselines − 2px) = 18px. line-height = exactly 2 baselines = 20px. Type is measured in baselines, not in points."
    }
  },

  "layout": {
    "--base-design-width": "980",
    "--layout-pw": "980px",
    "--layout-cn": "12",
    "--layout-cw": "65px",
    "--layout-gw": "10px",
    "--layout-mw": "45px",
    "--layout-bh": "10px",
    "_grid_check": "12 × 65 + 11 × 10 + 2 × 45 = 980. The grid closes exactly.",
    "--header-height": { "value": "calc(.0816327 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 80 },
    "--footer-height": { "value": "calc(.0663265 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 65 },
    "--footer-column": { "value": "calc(.219388 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 215 },
    "--page-height": "calc(100vh - var(--header-height))",
    "--image-height": { "value": "calc(.265306 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 260 },
    "--col-width": "calc(calc(0.0663265306122449 * var(--client-width)) * 12 + calc(0.0102041 * var(--client-width)) * 11)"
  },

  "spacing_desktop": {
    "--space-xs": { "value": "calc(.00510204 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 5 },
    "--space-sm": { "value": "calc(.0102041 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 10 },
    "--space-md": { "value": "calc(.0153061 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 15 },
    "--space-lg": { "value": "calc(.0229592 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 22.5 },
    "--space-xl": { "value": "calc(.0459184 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 45 },
    "--block-margin": "var(--space-xl)"
  },

  "radius": {
    "--field-border-radius": "0",
    "--tile-border-radius": "0",
    "--quantity-border-radius": "0",
    "--round": "9999px",
    "_note": "Everything rectangular is a hard 0. `--round: 9999px` exists only for pill/circle chrome (badges, icon buttons). There is no in-between radius anywhere in the system."
  },

  "borders": {
    "--border-width": "0.75px",
    "--border-color": "transparent",
    "--grid-divider": "calc(.00102041 * var(--client-width))",
    "--grid-divider-color": "var(--te-grey-300)",
    "_note": "Sub-pixel hairlines. Surfaces are separated by rules, not by shadows. There is no elevation system on this site."
  },

  "motion": {
    "--theme-dt": "0s",
    "--mrq-duration": "12s",
    "--mrq-gap": "40px",
    "--mrq-copies": "2",
    "--mrq-play-state": "paused",
    "_note": "The entire production CSS contains ONE cubic-bezier. There is no easing system, because there is essentially no motion. The only sanctioned movement is a 12s linear marquee. Treat stillness as a design decision, not an omission."
  },

  "controls": {
    "--btn-min-click-area": "48px",
    "--button-height-sm": { "value": "calc(.0357143 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 35 },
    "--button-height-md": { "value": "calc(.0510204 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 50 },
    "--button-height-lg": { "value": "calc(.0663265 * var(--client-width))", "px_at_980": 65 },
    "--field-font-size": "var(--fs-15)",
    "--field-line-height": "1.5em",
    "--field-border": "calc(.00102041 * var(--client-width)) solid var(--field-border-color)",
    "--field-background": "#fff",
    "--field-placeholder": "var(--te-grey-700)"
  },

  "z_index": {
    "--z-over": "100",
    "--z-navigation": "1000",
    "--z-modal-backdrop": "2000",
    "--z-dialog": "2001"
  }
}
